questions.question: What does a trunk link do?
choices: Connects a PC to a switch
questions.explanation: A trunk carries traffic for multiple VLANs between switches using 802.1Q.

correct.explanation: A trunk carries traffic for multiple VLANs between switches using 802.1Q.

incorrect.explanation: Carries one VLAN only misses what this question tests. A trunk carries traffic for multiple VLANs between switches using 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Carries one VLAN only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Connects a PC to a switch misses what this question tests. A trunk carries traffic for multiple VLANs between switches using 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Connects a PC to a switch" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Routes between VLANs misses what this question tests. A trunk carries traffic for multiple VLANs between switches using 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Routes between VLANs" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: A trunk is faster than an access port — A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link

questions.question: How big is the 802.1Q tag added to a frame?
questions.explanation: The 802.1Q tag is 4 bytes and contains the 12-bit VLAN ID.

correct.explanation: The 802.1Q tag is 4 bytes and contains the 12-bit VLAN ID.

incorrect.explanation: 2 bytes uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "2 bytes" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 8 bytes uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 8.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "8 bytes" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 12 bytes uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 12.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "12 bytes" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: A trunk is faster than an access port — A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link

questions.question: On an 802.1Q trunk, how is the native VLAN’s traffic sent?
questions.explanation: Native VLAN frames cross the trunk untagged.

correct.explanation: Native VLAN frames cross the trunk untagged.

incorrect.explanation: Double-tagged reflects a common trap: Thinking all VLANs are tagged. The native VLAN is untagged; all others are tagged.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Thinking all VLANs are tagged

incorrect.explanation: Tagged reflects a common trap: Thinking all VLANs are tagged. The native VLAN is untagged; all others are tagged.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Thinking all VLANs are tagged

incorrect.explanation: Dropped misses what this question tests. Native VLAN frames cross the trunk untagged.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing inside local, inside global, and outside addresses

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Mismatched native VLANs — Native VLAN must match on both ends or traffic can leak (CDP warns)

questions.question: Two trunked switches log a native VLAN mismatch. What is the likely effect?
choices: Traffic from those VLANs can leak/merge
questions.explanation: A native VLAN mismatch can merge the two native VLANs’ traffic and triggers a CDP warning.

correct.explanation: A native VLAN mismatch can merge the two native VLANs’ traffic and triggers a CDP warning.

incorrect.explanation: Trunk speed drops is a plausible symptom fix but does not match the most likely root cause here: A native VLAN mismatch can merge the two native VLANs’ traffic and triggers a CDP warning.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing inside local, inside global, and outside addresses

incorrect.explanation: All VLANs stop reflects a common trap: Mismatched native VLANs. Native VLAN must match on both ends or traffic can leak (CDP warns).
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Mismatched native VLANs

incorrect.explanation: Nothing — it is cosmetic is a plausible symptom fix but does not match the most likely root cause here: A native VLAN mismatch can merge the two native VLANs’ traffic and triggers a CDP warning.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing inside local, inside global, and outside addresses

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Mismatched native VLANs — Native VLAN must match on both ends or traffic can leak (CDP warns)

questions.question: Which command forces a port to trunk?
questions.explanation: switchport mode trunk sets the port to trunking.

correct.explanation: switchport mode trunk sets the port to trunking.

incorrect.explanation: switchport mode access reflects a common trap: A trunk is faster than an access port. A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A trunk is faster than an access port

incorrect.explanation: switchport trunk allowed vlan reflects a common trap: A trunk is faster than an access port. A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A trunk is faster than an access port

incorrect.explanation: switchport nonegotiate misses what this question tests. switchport mode trunk sets the port to trunking.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "switchport nonegotiate" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: A trunk is faster than an access port — A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link

questions.question: Which command disables DTP auto-negotiation on a port?
questions.explanation: switchport nonegotiate stops DTP, a hardening best practice.

correct.explanation: switchport nonegotiate stops DTP, a hardening best practice.

incorrect.explanation: no dtp misses what this question tests. switchport nonegotiate stops DTP, a hardening best practice.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "no dtp" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: switchport mode dynamic misses what this question tests. switchport nonegotiate stops DTP, a hardening best practice.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "switchport mode dynamic" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: no switchport trunk misses what this question tests. switchport nonegotiate stops DTP, a hardening best practice.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "no switchport trunk" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Leaving DTP on for untrusted ports — Disable DTP with switchport nonegotiate to prevent VLAN hopping

questions.question: Which command shows trunk mode, native VLAN, and allowed VLANs?
questions.explanation: show interfaces trunk lists trunking details.

correct.explanation: show interfaces trunk lists trunking details.

incorrect.explanation: show vlan brief misses what this question tests. show interfaces trunk lists trunking details.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show vlan brief" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: show ip interface brief misses what this question tests. show interfaces trunk lists trunking details.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show ip interface brief" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: show cdp neighbors misses what this question tests. show interfaces trunk lists trunking details.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "show cdp neighbors" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: A trunk is faster than an access port — A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link

questions.question: Which command limits a trunk to VLANs 10 and 20?
questions.explanation: switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20 prunes the trunk.

correct.explanation: switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20 prunes the trunk.

incorrect.explanation: switchport access vlan 10,20 reflects a common trap: A trunk is faster than an access port. A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A trunk is faster than an access port

incorrect.explanation: vlan 10,20 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 20 — not 10.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing VLAN tagging, trunking, and access ports

incorrect.explanation: switchport trunk native vlan 10 reflects a common trap: A trunk is faster than an access port. A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: A trunk is faster than an access port

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: A trunk is faster than an access port — A trunk simply carries multiple VLANs; speed depends on the physical link
